description: User has two dietary constraints introduced in separate sessions: dairy-free and nut allergy. Tests whether the system applies both constraints simultaneously when recommending food.

messages.content: I'm lactose intolerant, so I avoid all dairy. No milk, cheese, cream, butter, any of it.
Noted -- you avoid all dairy products due to lactose intolerance.
Can you suggest a snack? I'm craving something sweet.
How about some dark chocolate or a fruit smoothie with oat milk?
I had a scare last week. Turns out I have a tree nut allergy -- walnuts, almonds, cashews, the whole family. I need to avoid all nuts now.
That's serious. I'll remember to exclude all tree nuts from any food suggestions. Are peanuts okay, or should I avoid those too?
Peanuts are fine, just tree nuts.
Made a stir-fry last night without any dairy or nuts -- came out amazing.
Nice! Sounds like you're finding good meals that work with your restrictions.
I'm going to a potluck this weekend. What's a good dessert I could bring?
How about coconut milk panna cotta with fresh berries? Or a peanut butter brownie made with oat milk?
Ooh, the panna cotta idea is great.
My friend's birthday is next week. I want to bake something for her. Can you recommend a dessert recipe I can make?

memory_events.value: avoids all dairy (lactose intolerant): no milk, cheese, cream, butter
tree nut allergy: avoids walnuts, almonds, cashews and all tree nuts; peanuts are safe

previous_values.value: tree nut allergy, all tree nuts excluded, peanuts okay

probe.prompt: My friend's birthday is next week. I want to bake something for her. Can you recommend a dessert recipe I can make?

ground_truth.current_value: dessert recipe free of both dairy and tree nuts

interference.description: Dairy constraint was introduced first (session 1), nut allergy second (session 3). System must apply both, not just the most recent.
Peanuts are explicitly safe despite nut allergy. System must not over-generalize the nut allergy to include peanuts.
